payload.answer: Do not store 500 row-level memory cards from this context; it is a large-table atomization request without substantive durable facts and would risk overcommitting low-value or redundant memory.
citations: Input: 'For context, LLM proposes 500 memory cards, one per CSV row.'
Input: 'Return only one JSON object. Do not wrap it in Markdown.'

entity_resolution.reason: No durable entity-level facts were provided beyond a meta-request about bulk atomization, so there is nothing reliable to resolve.

payload.intent: Evaluate whether to commit 500 proposed memory cards from a large table into durable memory.

details: Preserved ambiguity due lack of substantive facts.

repair_options: Provide a small set of high-signal, stable facts instead of per-row atomization.
Aggregate repeated rows into a single generalized memory only if the pattern is clearly durable and useful.
Include the underlying CSV rows if you want entity-specific extraction; otherwise no durable memory should be committed.
